root_cause_summary: ImageMagick failed to enforce the module-domain policy (policy.xml: <policy domain="module" rights="none" pattern="..."/>) in three code paths responsible for loading and registering image coders. (1) RegisterStaticModules() in MagickCore/static.c had zero policy authorization before invoking register_module() for each built-in coder, so all static modules including Postscript (PS/EPI/EPS/EPSF/EPSI) were registered unconditionally at startup regardless of policy.xml restrictions. (2) RegisterStaticModule() used rights=ReadPolicyRights — a single-right check that only verified Read authorization, but a module policy with rights="none" should block both Read and Write. This single-right check was insufficient to prevent module registration when the admin intended to deny all access. (3) OpenModule() in MagickCore/module.c used the same insufficient rights=ReadPolicyRights, allowing dynamic module loading even under a "none" module policy. The consequence: Postscript files could be read and written even when explicitly excluded by a <policy domain="module" rights="none" pattern="PS" /> directive.
fix_summary: Commit 35893e7ca ("Added missing policy checks in RegisterStaticModules") added the missing IsRightsAuthorized guard to RegisterStaticModules() and changed RegisterStaticModule()'s rights from ReadPolicyRights to AllPolicyRights (an intermediate/overbroad step). Commit 01faddbe2 ("Use the correct rights") corrected all three functions: OpenModule(), RegisterStaticModule(), and RegisterStaticModules() to use rights=ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights instead of AllPolicyRights or ReadPolicyRights; it also added proper PolicyRights variable declaration and initialization in RegisterStaticModules(). The final fixed state across both commits is: every module registration/loading path checks IsRightsAuthorized(ModulePolicyDomain, ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights, module_name) before allowing the module to register or load.

source_refs.snippet: Added missing policy checks in RegisterStaticModules.

necessary_conditions: Administrator configures policy.xml with <policy domain="module" rights="none" pattern="..."/> to restrict a coder module
The restricted coder exists as a static built-in module (coders-list.h) or as a dynamically loadable module
ImageMagick startup calls RegisterStaticModules(), or runtime calls RegisterStaticModule()/OpenModule() for the restricted coder
vulnerable_code_patterns: RegisterStaticModules() iterates MagickModules[] array and calls register_module() for each unregistered module without any IsRightsAuthorized(ModulePolicyDomain, ...) guard

RegisterStaticModule() uses rights=ReadPolicyRights instead of rights=ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights in the IsRightsAuthorized call, only checking Read authorization when both Read and Write should be verified
OpenModule() uses rights=ReadPolicyRights instead of rights=ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights in the IsRightsAuthorized call, same single-right insufficiency

fix_code_patterns: IsRightsAuthorized(ModulePolicyDomain, ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights, module_name) guard added before register_module() call in RegisterStaticModules()
rights=ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights replaces rights=ReadPolicyRights (or intermediate AllPolicyRights) in RegisterStaticModule()
rights=ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights replaces rights=AllPolicyRights (or original ReadPolicyRights) in OpenModule()
PolicyRights variable declaration and initialization added to RegisterStaticModules()
added_guards: if (IsRightsAuthorized(ModulePolicyDomain,rights,module_name) == MagickFalse) { errno=EPERM; ... }  // rights corrected from ReadPolicyRights to ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights in RegisterStaticModule()
if (IsRightsAuthorized(ModulePolicyDomain,rights,module_name) == MagickFalse) { errno=EPERM; ... }  // rights corrected from AllPolicyRights to ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights in OpenModule()
removed_vulnerable_logic: AllPolicyRights removed as rights value in RegisterStaticModules() guard (01faddbe2)
FixEvidence.semantic_change: Module policy enforcement was added to RegisterStaticModules() and corrected in RegisterStaticModule() and OpenModule() to check both ReadPolicyRights and WritePolicyRights against the ModulePolicyDomain. This ensures that a <policy domain="module" rights="none"/> restriction blocks all module registration and loading, not just Read operations.

preconditions: User has configured policy.xml with module-domain restrictions
IsRightsAuthorized function must be reachable (requires policy.h inclusion)
configuration_guards: ModulePolicyDomain is the policy domain checked by IsRightsAuthorized
ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights are the rights bits that must be authorized for the module to load
version_or_feature_guards: MAGICKCORE_BUILD_MODULES: when defined, dynamic modules are used instead of static MagickModules[] array. The static.c path (RegisterStaticModules) is only used when MAGICKCORE_BUILD_MODULES is NOT defined.
OpenModule() is always compiled regardless of MAGICKCORE_BUILD_MODULES

hard_certificate_candidates.candidate: RegisterStaticModules without IsRightsAuthorized guard on MagickModules[i].register_module()
rights=ReadPolicyRights in RegisterStaticModule before IsRightsAuthorized call
rights=ReadPolicyRights in OpenModule before IsRightsAuthorized call
rights=ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights in IsRightsAuthorized(ModulePolicyDomain,...) in all three functions

admission_requirements: For NOT_AFFECTED: must show ModulePolicyDomain authorization check exists with rights covering both Read and Write in all three functions (OpenModule, RegisterStaticModule, RegisterStaticModules)
For FIXED: must show rights=ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights (or equivalent covering both) in IsRightsAuthorized(ModulePolicyDomain, ...)
For AFFECTED: must show either no IsRightsAuthorized guard in RegisterStaticModules, OR rights=ReadPolicyRights alone (without WritePolicyRights) in RegisterStaticModule/OpenModule

priority_only_evidence.reason: commit 1 added guard using AllPolicyRights (intermediate state, still not fully correct)
commit message confirms 'missing policy checks' in RegisterStaticModules

forbidden_hard_certificates: Token 'IsRightsAuthorized' in any file without ModulePolicyDomain context
Token 'ModulePolicyDomain' without verifying rights value (ReadPolicyRights vs ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights)
Token 'ReadPolicyRights' alone without checking it appears in a policy-domain authorization context
Function name 'RegisterStaticModule' without inspecting the rights value used in its policy check
CertificatePolicy.reason: cert_absent_allowed=true because the absence of the IsRightsAuthorized guard in RegisterStaticModules is directly observable and confirmable via git grep. cert_fixed_allowed=true because the presence of rights=ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights in the correct functions is a high-specificity fix token. Hard certificate candidates are concrete line-level patterns. Forbidden hard certificates guard against lazy evidence (file-touched, generic-function-name, or partial-token matches that fail to distinguish the vulnerable vs fixed state).

line_risk_signals.signal: RegisterStaticModules() calls register_module() without IsRightsAuthorized guard
rights=ReadPolicyRights (single right, missing WritePolicyRights) in RegisterStaticModule
rights=ReadPolicyRights (single right, missing WritePolicyRights) in OpenModule
Fix: IsRightsAuthorized(ModulePolicyDomain,rights,...) with rights=ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights covers both read and write
Fix: Missing policy guard added to RegisterStaticModules()
CWE-362 (Race Condition) does not align with patch evidence; no concurrent access pattern exists in the vulnerable or fixed code

uncertainty: CWE-362 (Race Condition) appears misassigned: the vulnerability exhibits no concurrent-execution or time-of-check-time-of-use pattern. The primary mechanism is missing/insufficient authorization (CWE-862/CWE-863). However, CWE-668 (Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere) correctly captures that the PS module is exposed to use when policy forbids it. CWE assignment is preserved as-is from case input but the race condition aspect has zero evidence in the patches.

The exact behavior of IsRightsAuthorized() with single-right vs multi-right arguments is not confirmed from available evidence. The fix assumes rights=ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights is the correct dual-right check. If IsRightsAuthorized uses a bitset AND semantics ('are ALL requested rights granted?'), then the original rights=ReadPolicyRights should have failed for a 'none' policy just as ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights would. The fact that the vulnerability existed despite having rights=ReadPolicyRights suggests the function may use different semantics, or the check was circumvented entirely through the RegisterStaticModules() path which had no guard at all.

Intermediate commits between 35893e7ca and 01faddbe2 (b344d1f10, 6920157d9, 8fa069797) were not inspected. These may contain additional context about why AllPolicyRights was introduced and then reverted to Read|Write.
